subject: Chemistry
title: Why Objects Have Color
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Why Objects Have Color.

hook.text: A red apple isn't red. It absorbs every color of light except red, which bounces back to your eyes.

buildup.text: White light contains all wavelengths. When it hits an object, atoms absorb certain wavelengths and reflect others.

discovery.text: Electrons in atoms jump to higher energy levels by absorbing specific light wavelengths. The rest bounce off.

twist.text: A black object absorbs all light and reflects none. A white object reflects all light and absorbs none.

climax.text: Color is not a property of objects—it's the light they reject. You see what an object doesn't want.

punchline.text: Objects absorb the colors they keep and reflect the colors they reject.

quiz.question: Why does a red apple appear red?
options: It absorbs all colors except red, which it reflects to your eyes
It emits red light from chemical reactions inside
Red pigment molecules glow when exposed to sunlight
